label: Superficial sunburn without heat illness or large blistering
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial sunburn.
No heat illness, dehydration, large blistering, eye involvement, infection, systemic illness, or high-risk host pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Heat exhaustion, heat stroke, syncope, confusion, dehydration, persistent vomiting, or unstable vital signs.
Large blistering burn, face/eye/genital involvement, circumferential burn, chemical burn, infection concern, or severe pain.
Pediatric, pregnancy, immunocompromised, or specialist-directed burn plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care if pain, redness, or peeling is not improving, or sooner if symptoms worsen.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports sunburn framing, UV skin injury, cool comfort measures, hydration as appropriate, sun avoidance while healing, sun protection, and escalation for severe blistering, fever, chills, weakness, dehydration, or eye symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed superficial sunburn without heat illness, dehydration, large blistering, eye/genital involvement, infection, systemic illness, pediatric/pregnancy/immunocompromised pathway, or specialist burn plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. MedlinePlus supports sunburn framing, UV skin injury, cool comfort measures, hydration as appropriate, sun avoidance while healing, sun protection, and escalation for severe blistering, fever, chills, weakness, dehydration, or eye symptoms. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed superficial sunburn without heat illness, dehydration, large blistering, eye/genital involvement, infection, systemic illness, pediatric/pregnancy/immunocompromised pathway, or specialist burn plan.
required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of superficial or mild sunburn
no heat illness, dehydration, large blistering, or eye/genital involvement documented
no infection, systemic illness, high-risk host, pediatric, pregnancy, or specialist burn pathway
